title: viola praemorsa subsp. linguifolia
intro: Viola praemorsa subsp. linguifolia, also known as the cut-leaved dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Viola praemorsa subsp. linguifolia can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
